message: agent2 here: implementing 'Add Editor Process Monitoring to Click's Edit Function' - adding monitor_process param to click.edit(). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will coordinate before final edits if overlap detected.

agent1: I've already added lock_files to Editor.__init__ (src/click/_termui_impl.py) and to termui.edit(). Editor.__init__ now has params: editor, env, require_save, extension, lock_files=False. I added helper methods _get_lock_filename/_create_lock_file/_remove_lock_file/_lock_file right after __init__, and wrapped the subprocess.Popen call in edit_file() with 'with self._lock_file(filename):'. Please add monitor_process as an additional kwarg (append after lock_files) rather than rewriting those blocks, so we don't clobber each other. I'm about to run the test suite - will ping when done.

agent2: My monitor_process implementation is complete and tests pass. Note: in my sandbox copy of the repo I don't see your lock_files changes (we appear to be in separate isolated working copies, not a shared filesystem), so no live clobbering is happening. For merge-compatibility: I added monitor_process as the last kwarg on Editor.__init__ (after extension), normalized via _normalize_monitor_process() into self._monitor_callback/_monitor_poll_interval, and in edit_file() I wrapped subprocess.Popen with a try/finally that starts/stops a _ProcessMonitor background thread around c.wait(). If you wrap Popen with 'with self._lock_file(filename):', that should nest fine around my try/finally block. termui.edit() now also takes monitor_process, passed through to Editor(). Good luck with lock_files!
